name: pyfun
description: Pyfun language support: syntax highlighting plus diagnostics, hover, navigation, and more, backed by `pyfun lsp`.

pyfun.server.path.description: Path to the `pyfun` executable used to launch the language server (`pyfun lsp`). Defaults to `pyfun` on PATH; during development point it at target/debug/pyfun.

textMateRules.comment: Declaration keywords `let`/`fun`/`type`/`measure`/`module` — the 'introduces a name' family, pinned to cyan (Monokai's `let` colour) so they read uniformly across themes. Control-flow keywords and the `mut`/`<-`/`extern` escape hatches stay pink.
`mut`/`<-`/`extern` — the mutability + FFI escape hatches, pinned to Monokai's keyword pink, matching Rust's `mut` (a `storage.modifier`).
Computation-expression builders `async`/`seq`/`result` — pinned to Monokai purple to give the CE openers their own identity, distinct from keywords and declarations.
Unit-of-measure annotations `<m>`, `<m/s^2>` — pinned to Monokai orange so dimensional units stand out from types and keywords.

Control-flow keywords (`if`/`then`/`else`/`elif`/`match`/`case`/`with`/`return`/`yield`/`do`/`in`) and the logical `and`/`or`/`not` — pinned to bracket-gold so control flow stays distinct and pink remains reserved for the `mut`/`<-`/`extern` escape hatches.
